signal: Feed-Score cron notification sent to wrong Discord channel. Root cause: GLM-5-turbo cannot reliably copy 19-digit channel IDs from prompt text when promptMode=minimal causes Messaging section to be skipped.
action: All 5 feed/notification cron prompts migrated from hardcoded channel IDs to env var approach (exec echo $DISCORD_CH_xxx). Systemic fix covers all current and future cron jobs.

signal: Model routing drift: feed-collect (0ead0a6f), feed-broadcast (9106f6c8), dev-mgr (32df77ec) running on claude-opus-4-6 despite being configured for zai/glm-5-turbo or astralor/claude-sonnet-4-6. Transition ~2026-03-29 03:00 UTC. Other GLM-configured jobs unaffected (heartbeat, balance monitors, thread-archiver, version-check). Possibly related to v3.28 upgrade.
action: Monitoring. Determine if persistent fallback or transient. Check ZAI provider status and v3.28 fallback behavior changes. If persistent, investigate why only these 3 jobs are affected while others route correctly.

signal: Feed-Score cron notification sent to wrong Discord channel. Root cause: GLM-5-turbo cannot reliably copy 19-digit channel IDs from prompt text when promptMode=minimal causes Messaging section to be skipped.
action: All 5 feed/notification cron prompts migrated from hardcoded channel IDs to env var approach (exec echo $DISCORD_CH_xxx). Systemic fix covers all current and future cron jobs.

signal: EXPANDED: Model routing drift is SYSTEMIC — ALL cron jobs (not just feed-collect/broadcast/dev-mgr) switched from configured model to Opus at ~2026-03-29 12:00 UTC, correlating with v3.28 upgrade. Thread-archiver (was GLM) and feed-broadcast (was Sonnet) also affected. Cron payload.model field is correctly set but being ignored by v3.28 runtime. Gateway config confirms agents.defaults.model.primary=Opus, agents.list[dev-mgr].model=GLM — but isolated cron sessions use the default, not the agent-specific or payload-specified model.
action: Escalate to human. This is a v3.28 behavior change (possibly breaking). Cron model override mechanism needs investigation. Current cost impact: ~5-30x increase for GLM-configured jobs. Options: (1) file upstream issue, (2) add per-session model overrides via session_status, (3) restructure cron jobs to use agentId routing if v3.28 respects agent-level model for cron.

signal: Env var migration: GLM back as model since ~19:28 CST. Version-check run at 20:28 CST sent to wrong channel (💓丨心跳 instead of 📝丨日志). 2 of 6 GLM runs succeeded, 4 showed 'Message failed' errors. Env var approach works but GLM compliance is intermittent — sometimes ignores exec echo instruction and saves to local file or picks wrong channel.
action: Monitor. Clean cycle count reset to 0 since prior 2 cycles were on Opus (not GLM). True validation requires 3 clean GLM cycles.

signal: SELF-CORRECTED. All checked jobs (version-check, feed-collect, thread-archiver) restored to GLM routing since ~19:28 CST March 30. Transition was transient (~24h during v3.28 migration). No manual intervention was needed. ZAI provider health confirmed normal.
action: Advance to clean cycle 1. If GLM routing holds for 2 more PCEC cycles, converge and archive.

signal: Env var channel migration: 8h since evt_217 reset. GLM routing restored, all feed-collect/feed-score runs completing successfully on GLM. No wrong-channel evidence in recent runs. version-check and thread-archiver also running without channel errors.
action: Advance to clean cycle 1. One more cycle needed for true GLM validation under sustained load.

signal: Model routing confirmed restored across all job types: feed-collect=GLM, feed-score=GLM, feed-broadcast=Sonnet, PCEC=Opus. 8+ hours of consistent correct routing since self-correction at ~19:28 CST March 30.
action: Advance to clean cycle 2. One more clean cycle to converge.
